Records.description: One-loop triangle in $D=2$ with 3 generic masses and 3 external momenta. Picard-Fuchs operator is order 1 with regular certificate. Certificate with extra poles leads to subtleties.

Records.authors: Ananthanarayan, B., Banik, Sumit, Friot, Samuel, Ghosh, Shayan
Records.description: Massive one-loop conformal 3-point (triangle) Feynman integral in dual-coordinate (x-space) representation with three distinct internal masses $m_1, m_2, m_3$ and generic (noninteger) propagator powers $a_1, a_2, a_3$ subject to the conformal constraint $a_1+a_2+a_3=D$. Evaluated via a threefold Mellin-Barnes representation using the conic hull method. Two sets of conformal variables (Set 1: $u,v,w$ for region A; Set 2: $u',v',w'$ for region B) yield 14 analytic continuation series representations each, built from 17 building blocks. Set 1 gives the Srivastava $H_C$ triple hypergeometric series and its analytic continuations in terms of $G_C$ and other triple series. Set 2 gives an alternative form involving half-integer arguments and Srivastava $H_B$ via a new quadratic transformation of $H_C$. Five independent series representations $S_1$-$S_5$ (resp. $S'_1$-$S'_5$) with explicit convergence regions $R_1$-$R_5$ (resp. $R'_1$-$R'_5$) are tabulated. Twelve spurious zeros of the Yangian bootstrap approach are identified and removed.

One-loop three-point function J^(3)(n;1,1,1) with three arbitrary internal masses m1, m2, m3 and three off-shell external momenta p1, p2, p3 (p1+p2+p3=0). At eps=0 the result is a sum of six rectangular-triangle integrals (Eq. 3.6) yielding the standard 't Hooft-Veltman form in terms of Cl_2; the eps-term is given as Li_3 (cf. ref. [NMB]). General higher orders in eps are presented as one-fold angular integrals (Eq. 3.6).

Results.resultTeXPreview: \left(\frac{m_3^2 m_2^2 \log \left(m_1^2 m_2^{-2}\right)}{\left(m_1^2-m_2^2\right) \left(m_1^2-m_3^2\right) \left(m_2^2-m_3^2\right)}-\frac{m_3^2 m_2^2 \log \left(m_1^2 m_3^{-2}\right)}{\left(m_1^2-m_2^2\right) \left(m_1^2-m_3^2\right) \left(m_2^2-m_3^2\right)}-\frac{m_1^2 m_2^2 \log \left(m_1^2 m_2^{-2}\right)}{\left(m_1^2-m_2^2\right) \left(m_1^2-m_3^2\right) \left(m_2^2-m_3^2\right)}+\frac{m_1^2 m_3^2 \log \left(m_1^2 m_3^{-2}\right)}{\left(m_1^2-m_2^2\right) \left(m_1^2-m_3^2\right) \left(m_2^2-m_3^2\right)}\right)+O\left(\varepsilon^1\right)
